page: Microsoft Azure F48ams_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famsv7 family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is allocated 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This configuration does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multiple compute and memory tasks. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top 10% performance in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, as well as Passmark CPU and physics evaluations. Memory performance is similarly strong, with write bandwidth for large blocks and read bandwidth for small cached blocks ranking in the top 10%. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the top 10%, with Redis reaching 130.96 million operations per second.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong memory bandwidth, the F48ams_v7 offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads. It is highly suitable for database operations, in-memory caching, and medium-scale language model inference, where it maintains top-tier and strong performance tiers. However, the lack of local storage requires external premium storage attachment, and the absence of a GPU limits its efficiency for large-scale deep learning training, as evidenced by average performance on 70B parameter LLM benchmarks.
description: Microsoft Azure F48ams_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Famsv7 family, featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 384.0 GB of RAM, translating to 8.0 GB per core. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but is premium storage capable. In benchmark testing, the F48ams_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng CPU tests, Passmark database operations, and Redis SET operations, which reach 130.96 million operations per second. It also shows strong memory bandwidth and efficient medium-model LLM inference. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, database management, and in-memory caching.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48ams_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier database and memory performance under Hyper-V virtualization, with no local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48ams_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and top-tier database performance.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server featuring forty-eight dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-bandwidth system memory for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmarks
No local storage or GPU, with premium network storage capability
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching, and medium LLM inference
